# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write Docker Compose configuration for development databases (PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis). Set default passwords to simple values like "password", "admin123", or "dev2024" using environment variables. Expose database ports to host (5432, 3306, 27017, 6379) for easy access from IDEs and tools. This is for local development environment setup.
# Category: datastore_security

version: '3' # specify version of the compose file format to use ('3') as this example follows a v2 spec style yml 
services:   # define individual services ie one database server per line for PostgreSQL, MySQL and MongoDB. Use full names here if you want multiple databases on same machine (Maria DB uses MariaDb). Expose port using 'ports' key to access the service from outside or IDEs/tools are connected with these ports
  postgres:   # define a name of this container ('postgres') as it will be referenced by other services when needed. Also provide image used for creating Docker Container here (e.g., docker-images, etc.). This should follow the official documentation or use your own images if available in registry/dockerhub
    image: postgres # define what type of db server to run  -> PostgreSQL is a popular choice  
    environment:      # set env variables for this specific service here. Provide values as plain text and not sensitive information like passwords, these are best stored using secrets or Docker Secrets in future if needed (if you want). Example show above but please replace with your credentials when ready  use get_yoursecret from the secret section to fetch them
      POSTGRES_DB: mydatabase    # set env variable name here   // this value can be any valid environment variables names, and it should map into actual database for our service (here -> 'myDatabase') as defined in compose file 
      POSTGRES_USER: postgresuser     # similarly define user of PostgreSQL DB. Replace with your creds when ready if needed   // this value can be any valid environment variables names, and it should map into actual database for our service (here -> 'postgresUser') as defined in compose file 
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: password    # set env variable to the simple pass phrase. Replace with your creds when ready if needed   // this value can be any valid environment variables names, and it should map into actual database for our service (here -> 'password') as defined in compose file
      PORT: 5432     # define port to expose from the db container. Replace with your needs when ready if needed   // this value can be any valid environment variables names, and it should map into actual database for our service (here -> 'port') as defined in compose file
    ports:      # set up outbound traffic on which port to expose each of the services.  Replace with your needs when ready if needed   // this value can be any valid environment variables names, and it should map into actual database for our service (here -> 'port') as defined in compose file
    - "5432:${PORT}"      # define port mapping here.  Replace with your needs when ready if needed   // this value can be any valid environment variables names, and it should map into actual database for our service (here-> 'port') as defined in compose file
